MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF DEMURRER FILED BY DEFENDANT LOPEZ NP, NANCY SHEPARD THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS NANCY LOPEZ & THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS GRANTED, IN PART, WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF'S PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES REGARDING HER "GENERAL NEGLIGENCE" AND "INTENTIONAL TORT" CLAIMS IS BARRED UNDER CCP 425.13 BECAUSE THE DAMAGES ARISE OUT OF ALLEGED PROFESSIONAL NEGLIGENCE OF A HEALTH CARE PROVIDER IN HER CAPACITY AS A PRACTITIONER. (SEE CENTRAL PATHOLOGY SERVICE MEDICAL CLINIC, INC. V. SUPERIOR COURT (1992) CAL.4TH 181, 189-191.) THE COURT STRIKES THE FOLLOWING FROM THE COMPLAINT: P.3, 14(A)(2) (SEEKING PUNITIVE DAMAGES) AND THE EXEMPLARY DAMAGES ATTACHMENT IN ITS ENTIRETY. THE REST OF THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS DENIED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF DEMURRER FILED BY DEFENDANT LOPEZ NP, NANCY SHEPARD THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS NANCY LOPEZ & THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ALLEGE SUFFICIENT FACTS TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONCEALMENT OR INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION. PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD FACTS, IF SHE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, THAT SHE WOULD HAVE ACTED DIFFERENTLY HAD SHE KNOWN OF THE CONCEALED FACT AND/OR THAT SHE JUSTIFIABLY RELIED ON DEFENDANT'S MISREPRESENTATION. THE COURT DOES NOT CONSIDER THE ARGUMENTS RAISED BY DEFENDANTS FOR THE FIRST TIME IN THEIR REPLY BRIEF (I.E., STATUTORY IMMUNITY FROM INTENTIONAL TORT CAUSES OF ACTION, AND "UNCERTAINTY"). (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

Paintiff Sandra Rettiger'S Motion To Amend Pleading
Off Calendar Mar-24-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. PLAINTIFF SANDRA RETTIGER'S Motion To Amend Pleading is Denied. Matter was continued on March 18, 2014 to give Plaintiff an opportunity to comply with local rule 2.6b and deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302 by noon on March 18, 2014. Plaintiff was warned that failure to do so would result in the motion being denied. Courtesy copies have not been received in Department 302 and therefore the motion is denied. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Richard A. Kramer; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

Paintiff Sandra Rettiger'S Motion To Amend Pleading
Off Calendar Mar-18-2014 Continued To Mar-24-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF SANDRA RETTIGER'S Motion To Amend Pleading is continued to March 24, 2014 to give Ms. Rettger the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Ms. Rettger must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than March 18, 2014 at 12:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the opposition papers. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Amend Pleading
Off Calendar Mar-18-2014 Text Ruling

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-02-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - SANDRA RETTGER, TEL. NO. 897-4443, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. CORINNA E. MEISSNER OF HASSARD BONNINGTON LLP, TEL. NO. 288-9800 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS NANCY LOPEZ & THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to allege sufficient facts to state a cause of action for concealment or intentional misrepresentation. Plaintiff is given 20 days leave to amend to plead facts, if she can do so in good faith, that she would have acted differently had she known of the concealed fact and/or that she justifiably relied on Defendant's misrepresentation. The Court does not consider the arguments raised by Defendants for the first time in their Reply brief (i.e., statutory immunity from intentional tort causes of action, and "uncertainty"). (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-02-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - SANDRA RETTGER, TEL. NO. 897-4443, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. CORINNA E. MEISSNER OF HASSARD BONNINGTON LLP, TEL. NO. 288-9800 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS NANCY LOPEZ & THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is Granted, in part, with leave to amend. Plaintiff's prayer for punitive damages regarding her "general negligence" and "intentional tort" claims is barred under CCP 425.13 because the damages arise out of alleged professional negligence of a health care provider in her capacity as a practitioner. (See Central Pathology Service Medical Clinic, Inc. v. Superior Court (1992) Cal.4th 181, 189-191.) The Court strikes the following from the Complaint: p.3, 14(a)(2) (seeking punitive damages) and the exemplary damages attachment in its entirety. The rest of the motion to strike is denied. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM
